summary: Backend testing for SHREE RAJ & CO CA-firm register completed. 13/13 pytest tests passed against the public EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Verified: (1) Document model uses 'last_entry_date' (deadline_date removed) and new 'uploaded_to_accounting' boolean; (2) POST/PUT /api/documents and POST/PUT /api/clients accept and persist all updated fields; (3) POST /api/ai/generate-message returns non-streaming JSON {message, client} with plain text (no markdown); (4) POST /api/ai/generate-cheatsheet returns plain text {content, client}; (5) POST /api/ai/daily-summary returns plain text {summary, date, stats}; (6) Existing CRUD for clients/documents/tasks still works. AI outputs were scanned for markdown patterns (**, *, _, #) and none found.

minor.issue: Still computes documents.required via db.documents.count_documents({'status': 'required'}) and returns 'required' in response, but the requirement removed the 'required' status (only 'pending' and 'submitted' should exist). Stale field. Reference: /app/backend/server.py:545,568

action_items: Optional cleanup: Remove the 'required' status counter from GET /api/dashboard/stats (server.py:545,568) since only 'pending' and 'submitted' statuses are valid now.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated datetime.utcnow() in many places (Pydantic Field defaults and AI endpoints). Consider datetime.now(timezone.utc) in future updates.

AI endpoints still loop over stream events to assemble a full text response; could use a direct send_message call if available in emergentintegrations for simpler code, but streaming-to-string works correctly.
Dashboard stats returns 'required' documents count even though the 'required' status was removed per requirements (minor staleness).
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed clients/documents/tasks and clean them up via DELETE in fixtures. No persistent seed data left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public URL = EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env (https://ca-register-hub.preview.emergentagent.com). AI endpoints take ~5-7s each, so allow generous timeouts. Plain-text validator in test file uses regex patterns for **, *, _, #. The 'required' status counter in /api/dashboard/stats is a leftover (minor).
